title: Rei in Episode 15
wikitext:
[]: Rei in Episode 15             Quote Post by schismatics » Tue Jan 26, 2010 1:27 pm So when Gendo is leaving the cemetery in Episode 15 why is Rei inside the helicopter thing? IIRC, Gendo's never taken Rei anywhere outside NERV HQ. Just wondering...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jan 26, 2010 1:45 pm I think it's made fairly clear that Gendo spends an awful lot more time with Rei than with Shinji. Also, something you should consider is what effect seeing Rei in the VTOL would have on Shinji. To me, I think it reminds Shinji to that he must reach out to his father in order to have a relationship with the same sort of proximity that Rei has with him.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Jan 26, 2010 1:46 pm Gendou keeps Rei close. The two are frequently seen together around Nerv. As to why she is in the VTOL, I tend to think it is because Gendou wanted her there. It is a relatively emotional day for him and perhaps Rei is there as a source of comfort. I imagine that Gendou was both dropped off and picked up by the VTOL, and Rei was inside the whole time. For various reasons, Gendou has elected not to take her to the grave itself.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jan 26, 2010 1:53 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: For various reasons, Gendou has elected not to take her to the grave itself.

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Tue Jan 26, 2010 1:57 pm As to why she is in the VTOL, I tend to think it is because Gendou wanted her there. It is a relatively emotional day for him and perhaps Rei is there as a source of comfort.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Jan 26, 2010 2:00 pm I’m sure Gendo and Rei probably go to lots of places outside of NERV together, it’s just not shown in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jan 26, 2010 3:26 pm child of Lilith wrote: I’m sure Gendo and Rei probably go to lots of places outside of NERV together, it’s just not shown in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jan 26, 2010 5:46 pm child of Lilith wrote: I’m sure Gendo and Rei probably go to lots of places outside of NERV together

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Jan 26, 2010 11:23 pm I think the main difference between Rei and Shinji in Gendo’s eyes is that Rei is “safe” where as Shinji is not. Gendo probably feels he can interact with Rei far more easily then with Shinji, so he treats Rei better and does whatever he can to push Shinji away. His pushing Shinji away isn’t because he hates him; it’s just that he doesn’t know how to relate to him on a personal level. That’s why he keeps him at arms length. It’s his way of avoiding a problem he doesn’t know how to deal with.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 27, 2010 12:13 am Rei = Reminds Gendo of Yui = Good Shinji = Reminds Gendo of himself = Not Good

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jan 27, 2010 12:23 am ^ That would be my assumption as well. Gendo definately strikes me as a self-hater.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Jan 27, 2010 12:55 am I’m sure he has plenty of reasons to hate himself. Caring out his plan to get Yui back defiantly caused him to make some pretty dark choices along the way. He’d have to be an unfeeling machine not to feel some level of hate towards himself because of that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 27, 2010 2:00 am Gendo's self-hatred (practically spelled out in 26') is probably a very old problem that results in him living a self-fulfilling prophecy. He expects other people to dislike him, so he (perhaps not knowing any other way to be) 'makes himself' unlikable. He doesn't think he deserves love, so he walls off his heart or runs away from those who might give it to him. Since Gendo is an NGE character, something clearly went very wrong in his childhood, and it probably involves a Shitty Father.

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Wed Jan 27, 2010 2:04 am I always thought Rei was piloting the craft. It's understandable if Gendo trusts only Rei to transport him to places outside the base (though he did go to Antarctica without her, and he was on the plane with the head of the Jet Alone project). As for his dismissal of Shinji, just because Shinji's a 14-year-old boy doesn't mean the person who is incontestably the most powerful figure in his life isn't intimidated by him. It could be because Shinji reminds Gendo of himself, something he doesn't like, and it could be because Gendo feels in the deepest parts of his mind that someone like him can't be and has no right to be a good parent, or even a parent. It could be both.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Wed Jan 27, 2010 2:19 am I think Rei was sitting in the passenger seat. I agree that Gendo’s hatred towards him self is probably long standing. Yui probably brought this under control, but as soon as she was gone it came raging back.
